# Transcrição: 09-When The Pixel Doesn't Show 

- **Fonte:** 09-When The Pixel Doesn't Show 
- **Duração:** 02:03
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:03]

Okay, so sometimes you'll be using the Facebook tool helper and you'll notice that you may be checking out a potential prospect or a client and you'll notice that the pixel is not installed on the website. So it's not unknown or it's not unheard of that certain tag managers will actually result in the pixel not being picked up by the pixel tool helper, such as Google tag manager can sometimes do this and other tag managers as well. So it's not really that common, but sometimes with bigger brands who have a lot of different agency partners, this can happen. So before we go in and pitch a client, hey, you know, all of these great things I can do I can help you out with your pixel strategy. Da da da. What we want to do is just double check that they in fact do not have the pixel installed. So this is Amazon.com, for example, this is one of the biggest websites on the planet. And you'll see here the pixel tool helper isn't picking up anything on this website. So what we would want to do just to confirm before we assume that there was no pixel here is check the page source. And what we're going to do is inspect the elements on this page. We're going to click on network and it's going to show us the network in about a minute and you'll see there's no Facebook here at all. You can also check the page source and on a Mac it's command F type in Facebook and you'll see nothing shows up versus say for example my website. If we go and check this page source and I type in Facebook, you can see the pixel is in fact on this website. Now this is Amazon.com the arch enemy of Facebook. So that's not surprising guys. But essentially this is how you would double check that the Facebook pixel is in fact installed or not installed on a website.
